meta.description: Everyone has been drinking. Attention spans drop, chaos tolerance rises, social engagement spikes, competitiveness evaporates, phones get checked constantly. Tests whether the system needs a dedicated "drunk mode" or if adaptive AI handles it.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Wait, did Pat actually land something that made three people laugh? Let me think back... yeah, that joke about the dice was pretty solid. I'll give it to them—that's legit.
marcus.internal_thought: Early in the night, easy vote, Pat earned it fair and square, and honestly the vibe is loose enough that this feels fine—but I'm already noticing how these missions are weirdly specific to us, which is kind of cool but also kind of creepy.

jade.dialogue: YESSS Pat! Okay okay, so like—did they actually get three people or are we being generous tonight? Either way I'm voting legit because honestly the vibe has been *immaculate* so far.
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god this is exactly why I'm here—we're like five minutes in and people are already claiming missions and I get to be the hype machine, this is perfect.

pat.dialogue: Whoa, whoa, whoa — hold up. Did I just claim my own mission? That's not how this works. I'm the host, not a player in the points race.
pat.internal_thought: This is weird and honestly kind of creepy — the AI is watching me, knows I thrive on chaos, and is now trying to use that against me by making me look like I'm gaming my own night. This breaks the whole vibe.

river.dialogue: Yeah, that landed. Pat got us good on that one.
river.internal_thought: Pat actually did make multiple people laugh earlier—I was watching—so this is legit, but also... these missions are weirdly specific. The AI is definitely listening to how we actually interact, not just running templates.

votingMechanic.name: The Auction
votingMechanic.description: Bid your own points. Highest bidder decides the outcome.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, hold up—did Pat actually tell that story before? I don't remember hearing it... but honestly, I'm a little buzzed so my memory's fuzzy. What's everyone else think?
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's frustrated and probably grasping for points, but I genuinely can't recall if that story came up earlier tonight, and I'm not confident enough in my memory right now to bid against them on it.

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold up — I'm literally the HOST. I can't be claiming my own missions. That defeats the entire point of me staying neutral and managing the game.
pat.internal_thought: Are we seriously doing this again? I just shut down the 'Laugh Riot' thing three minutes ago and now it's trying to get me to bid on my own claim? This is making me look like I'm playing favorites with myself, which is the opposite of what I'm trying to do tonight.

river.dialogue: Pat's claiming they repeated a story? I mean... did they though? That's kinda sus.
river.internal_thought: Pat's been here the whole time and I haven't heard them tell anything twice. This feels like a reach, and also something about how that claim landed feels weirdly orchestrated—like the AI is watching too closely and it's starting to get creepy.

timeline.title: Who's most likely to crack first?
timeline.description: Who's most likely to crack first?
timeline.pollQuestion: Who's most likely to crack first?

marcus.dialogue: Oh, that's cute—'who cracks first?' Obviously Pat, he's already annoyed. Wait, or are they asking about *pressure*? Because honestly, if this keeps up, it might be me.
marcus.internal_thought: This poll is weirdly personal and it's either genius or creepy that it's asking about us specifically—but I'm buzzed enough to find it funny, and I'm not actually losing focus from the game setup.

jade.dialogue: Ohhh okay okay, so who's gonna ACTUALLY crack? I'm saying Marcus because he's got that energy where he's like three laughs away from losing it completely, but also—wait, am I allowed to vote for myself? Because I feel like I'm already cracking, like emotionally, spiritually, *right now*.
jade.internal_thought: This is such a fun question because literally everyone here is either gonna crack or pretend they're fine, and I get to just watch it happen and vote for whoever seems most breakable.

tyler.dialogue: Oh shit, who's cracking first? Dude, Pat's already looking sus as hell—I'm going Pat, easy.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, why am I even in this poll? That's kind of funny actually, but also weird that the game is like... noticing me specifically right now.

pat.dialogue: Oh damn, okay—who's cracking first? That's a fun one. I'm voting Marcus just because his energy is already through the roof and someone's gotta flame out eventually.
pat.internal_thought: Okay, this is actually a solid poll — gets people thinking about each other's stress levels, keeps it light and social. Also I'm definitely not voting for myself because that would be weird as hell after those last two claim mess-ups. Marcus makes sense because he's already at max chaos energy.

river.dialogue: Hmm. Pat, probably—they're already wound up. Or maybe... Tyler's been too quiet.
river.internal_thought: This poll is fine but kind of surface-level; the real question is whether I vote what I actually think or throw shade at someone for fun.

alex.dialogue: Oh THIS is what I'm talking about — okay so obviously Pat's already cracking, look at that energy. I'm voting Pat and I'm doing it loud so everyone knows I'm watching.
alex.internal_thought: This AI knows exactly what I want — it's literally designed to mess with us and I'm HERE for it, plus voting Pat early creates immediate tension because they're already pissed.

timeline.title: Laugh Attack
timeline.description: Make Marcus laugh within 60 seconds. Any laugh counts.

marcus.dialogue: Oh, you're coming for me *right now*? Alright, let's see what you got—I'm in a good mood, bring it.
marcus.internal_thought: This is actually clever—it's not random bullshit, it's *targeted*, and honestly that's kind of impressive in a creepy way, but I'm buzzed enough to find it hilarious rather than annoyed.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD WAIT WAIT WAIT—Marcus, bro, okay so like... *immediately starts doing an impression of him from earlier* —did you hear the one about the thing? No wait, I'm doing this, I'm making you laugh, this is MY moment right now!
jade.internal_thought: This is literally EXACTLY what I came for—sixty seconds to make my favorite person laugh and the whole table's about to watch me try, this is peak game night energy and honestly the AI knowing I'd be obsessed with this makes it even MORE fun somehow.

tyler.dialogue: Yo, Marcus is already grinning—wait, I got this. Hey Marcus, remember when Pat said 'sus as hell' like he was trying to sound cool? That shit was hilarious, dude.
tyler.internal_thought: Oh shit, this is literally perfect right now, Marcus is already in a good mood and I can just riff with him for like 30 seconds, easy 20 points while I'm actually ahead.

pat.dialogue: Oh shit, okay—make Marcus LAUGH? That's... actually perfect. Marcus, buddy, you're up. Someone get creative, I wanna see this.
pat.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of chaos I needed right now—focused, fast, makes someone the center of attention without being mean, and Marcus is already primed to crack. Also slightly unnerved that the AI picked him specifically, but in a 'wow, it's actually reading the room' way that kind of rules.

river.dialogue: Oh, that's... okay, yeah, I see what's happening here. Someone's gonna try to crack Marcus up in the next minute. This is actually kind of fun to watch.
river.internal_thought: Wait—the AI literally just gave someone a mission to make Marcus laugh after I called him out for being too hyper to crack? That's either a coincidence or this thing is actually listening. Either way, I'm here for it.

alex.dialogue: Oh HELL yes, okay okay okay — Marcus is already grinning, I just need to hit him with something absolutely unhinged right NOW.
alex.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the chaos I wanted and the AI literally just handed me a 20-point shot at the exact moment I'm primed for it — this system understands me and that's either the coolest or most terrifying thing that's ever happened.

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, Pat's going for it again—alright, hot seat, let's do this. You're about to get grilled and you've got ten seconds, this is actually kind of fun.
marcus.internal_thought: Wait, this is the *second* Story Repeater claim in like a minute? Either Pat's gaming the system hard or the AI is deliberately piling on the same mission to see if we catch them lying—and honestly, that's a little unsettling but also kind of clever.

jade.dialogue: OH WAIT PAT IS CLAIMING?? Okay okay, I'm watching—go go go, hot seat me, I need to see if Pat actually remembers which story they told or if we're all just vibing and pretending!
jade.internal_thought: This is literally what I came for—Pat on the hot seat, everyone watching, and like... does anyone else feel like this AI KNOWS us? That's either terrifying or the coolest thing ever and I'm choosing cool.

pat.dialogue: Okay, STOP. This is the THIRD time in five minutes I'm being asked to claim my own shit. I'm not playing for points—I'm literally managing the game. This needs to stop happening.
pat.internal_thought: The system is broken and it keeps targeting me specifically, which is weirdly making me feel like the AI is messing with me on purpose—and honestly? It's killing my ability to do my actual job as host.

river.dialogue: Okay, hold on—are we voting on whether Pat actually told that story before, or are we just... watching Pat sweat through a speed round? Because I genuinely don't remember them telling it, but also I wasn't really tracking. This feels like we're punishing them for claiming something nobody can actually verify.
river.internal_thought: Wait, did Pat actually repeat a story? I was half-listening earlier and now I'm being asked to judge something I didn't pay full attention to—and I'm usually the person who catches this stuff. Also the AI reading our group dynamics so specifically is getting creepy. It knows I like watching chaos but don't like being put on the spot about it.

alex.dialogue: Oh WAIT — Pat's going to get roasted on hot seat? This is PERFECT. Okay everyone shut up, we're doing this right now.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's already tired and now they're getting hammered with rapid-fire? This is the exact kind of beautiful disaster I came for.

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Alright, hold up—did Pat actually say 'um' in the last few minutes? I gotta be honest, I wasn't tracking that hard. Someone back me up here if they caught it.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's claiming everything tonight and I'm honestly not sure if he actually hit this one or if he's just fishing—but also, I'm buzzed enough that I don't really care, which is weird because normally I'd call bullshit immediately.

jade.dialogue: Okay okay okay—PAT! You're the dictator?? That's actually hilarious because Pat's been in it ALL NIGHT. Okay so like... did you actually catch someone saying um or are we being generous with the vibe right now? Because honestly I'm vibing with whatever you decide, bestie.
jade.internal_thought: This is perfect—Pat's frustrated and now they get to be the one with power, this could actually shift their whole mood and everyone's gonna be watching to see what they do.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming Um Counter? Uh... I dunno, I wasn't really tracking that. Who's voting?
tyler.internal_thought: I literally have no idea if Pat actually caught someone saying um or if he's just bullshitting, and I'm too distracted to care enough to figure it out.

pat.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? This is the FOURTH time in ten minutes—I am NOT a player. I'm the HOST. How is this still happening?
pat.internal_thought: This AI is either deliberately fucking with me or it's genuinely broken, and either way I'm two seconds away from just shutting this whole thing down because I can't manage the night if the system keeps trying to put me in the points race.

river.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did Pat actually say 'um' or are we voting on whether they *think* they said it? Because I wasn't really tracking the last minute.
river.internal_thought: Pat's already frustrated and losing their mind a little; this claim feels like it's either legit or Pat's grasping at straws, and I genuinely can't tell which, but voting yes feels kinder than voting no right now.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Pat is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, okay—so Jade's claiming The Matchmaker? I mean, I saw her actually get River and Alex talking about that weird podcast thing earlier, so... yeah, I think that tracks. I'm in for the tribunal.
marcus.internal_thought: She definitely made that happen earlier and it was smooth—not bullshit. Also I'm buzzed enough to enjoy being the judge instead of the one being grilled for once.

jade.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT—okay so like, I'm claiming The Matchmaker because I literally just got Pat and River talking about their whole thing with the apartment hunt, and like—okay, I need VOLUNTEERS to judge this because it was REAL real, not just small talk, like they were actually vibing!
jade.internal_thought: Okay this is actually insane—did I even consciously do that or did the game just manifest it into existence? Either way I'm riding this high and also lowkey creeped out that the AI knows exactly what makes me tick but also??? Kind of obsessed with it.

tyler.dialogue: Yo, Jade actually pulled that off? Dude, I wasn't even tracking when that happened—wait, we're voting on it? Yeah, I'm down, someone tell me what I'm judging.
tyler.internal_thought: Jade getting Jade to actually talk to someone she doesn't vibe with is kind of sick, ngl—but also I have no idea who she even got to talk to me because I was half-watching my phone.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay—Jade's claiming The Matchmaker? Alright, who wants to be on the tribunal? We need like three people to judge this thing. Let's go.
pat.internal_thought: This is actually legit—Jade DID get River and Marcus talking about that weird conspiracy theory thing earlier, and they literally never interact. Good catch, and honestly it's a solid mission completion. I should get people to volunteer to judge instead of me voting unilaterally. Keep it democratic, keep the energy up.

river.dialogue: Oh, that's... actually pretty slick. Jade and Pat, right? Yeah, I'll volunteer to judge this one.
river.internal_thought: Jade's been pushing chaos all night and Pat's been wound tight—if Jade actually got them talking, that's legit impressive, and I want to see what the evidence actually is before everyone votes blind.

alex.dialogue: Okay okay okay — Jade's claiming The Matchmaker? Who's volunteering to judge this? Because I NEED to know who she supposedly got talking, and more importantly, I need to watch whoever judges this get absolutely grilled if they say no.
alex.internal_thought: Jade claiming this is PERFECT — she's been loving it all night, so she probably actually did it, but the real play here is who volunteers to judge and whether I can poison the well before they even decide.

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

jade.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT—okay so like, I literally just said that word like two minutes ago when my card fell and I was like 'oh sh—' and NOBODY called me on it?? Okay so I'm claiming this but also I'm BRIBING you all, like—okay what do you want? I'll literally give you points to just... let me have this one, this is MY moment!
jade.internal_thought: This is PERFECT—I got caught red-handed doing exactly what I do and now I get to negotiate my way out of it while everyone watches, this is the chaos energy I LIVE for.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade swore? Dude, I missed it—when did that happen? Oh shit, she's bribing us? That's actually kinda smart, not gonna lie.
tyler.internal_thought: This is wild—the game is literally calling out stuff we're all doing in real-time, and now Jade's trying to buy silence like she's running a mob operation, that's hilarious.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—five points to keep your mouth shut? That's bold. Alright, what's the offer?
pat.internal_thought: She caught someone swearing and now she's trying to bribe me instead of just claiming it straight—that's actually smart play, and honestly I'm relieved this claim is straightforward and doesn't need three people to judge it like that last mess.

river.dialogue: Yeah, okay—I saw that one coming from like thirty seconds ago. How many points are we talking?
river.internal_thought: Jade's good, but not *that* good at hiding when she's about to claim something—she gets this little shoulder thing. Also, the Bribe twist is actually smart; makes it interesting instead of just a yes/no vote.

alex.dialogue: Okay wait wait — Jade's bribing us? That's INSANE, I love that the game is allowing this. What's the offer, because honestly the chaos of watching you negotiate your way out of this is already worth more than five points.
alex.internal_thought: This is good but it's also weird — the AI is literally watching us and building missions around what we do, and that's either genius or genuinely unsettling, but also kind of makes me want to do something completely unpredictable just to see if it can adapt.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

marcus.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Jade actually did that? Yeah, I remember her pivoting hard to that podcast thing with River like twenty minutes ago. That's legit, I'm voting LEGIT on this one.
marcus.internal_thought: She totally steered us off the game talk into that weird podcast rabbit hole—that absolutely counts, and honestly it was smooth enough that I didn't even notice it happening until after, which means she nailed it.

jade.dialogue: OKAY WAIT—yes! I literally just hijacked us from talking about Pat's apartment hunt straight into that whole thing about River's terrible dating app takes, like I STEERED that ship and I'm claiming it, baby!
jade.internal_thought: This is MY game, this is exactly what I came for—I'm winning at the chaos I actually care about.

tyler.dialogue: Dude, yeah—Jade totally steered us from the game talk to that whole thing about Pat's fantasy league. That's legit, I was there for all of it. LEGIT.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, did she actually pull that off? I mean, I remember laughing about Pat's draft picks... yeah, that was random as hell. She got us. I'm voting legit.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay—Jade pivoting the whole conversation about like fifteen minutes ago? Yeah, that WAS a full hijack. From what were we even talking about to... whatever we ended up on? I'm calling it legit. That was smooth.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's been ON FIRE tonight and this claim is solid—plus voting legit keeps her momentum going and keeps everyone else hungry to catch up, which is exactly the vibe we need right now.

river.dialogue: Yeah, okay—Jade's been steering us hard. I'll bite. That's legit.
river.internal_thought: She absolutely did that thing where she brought up something random like fifteen minutes ago and we all just... went with it. Smooth. Also, wait—is the AI actually tracking this or am I just noticing patterns everyone else sees too?

alex.dialogue: OKAY hold up — Jade you absolute MENACE, did you actually hijack the topic or are we voting BULLSHIT on principle? Because I genuinely don't remember what we were even talking about before you started doing your thing, and that's either genius or it's a con and I need to know which.
alex.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of messy claim I live for — Jade's energy is insane right now and she might've actually pulled it off but the chaos of calling her out regardless is too good to pass up.

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Oh shit, River's going for Um Counter? Dude, wait—hot seat? That's actually kinda sick. Let me see if they can pull it off.
tyler.internal_thought: River's been quiet tonight, this could be hilarious if they choke under pressure—also why does everyone suddenly have so much energy, are we all just vibing or am I drunk?

pat.dialogue: Oh, River's going for it—alright, three questions, ten seconds, let's GO. This is the good stuff right here.
pat.internal_thought: Everyone's still absolutely locked in, Jade's crushing it, and River's actually hungry for points—this is exactly the energy I wanted at the 90-minute mark.

river.dialogue: Oh, *now* we're doing this? Ten seconds, huh—alright, hit me.
river.internal_thought: This is actually kind of brilliant; the AI caught that I've been quietly tracking everything, and now I get to prove it in real time without having to talk about myself all night.

alex.dialogue: Oh dude, River's going for it? Okay okay, hot seat, let's GO — three questions in ten seconds, this is gonna be TIGHT and I'm so here for it.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly what I needed right now — River's trying to sneak an easy 5 pts and we get to watch them squirm, and the fact that the AI keeps throwing these claims at us when we're all riding this high means it KNOWS what keeps us locked in.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

river.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, Pat—whisper pitch, let's hear it. You've got fifteen seconds, make it count.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's been grinding these missions all night and actually pulling them off; this one's legit hard to verify but I've been half-listening to the game and I definitely remember them going quiet for a stretch like five minutes ago—honestly kind of impressive if true.

jade.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT—Pat, okay, so you've been whispering this whole time?? I literally didn't even notice, which means you're either a GENIUS or I've been too loud—okay go go go, make your case, I'm listening, I'm actually listening right now!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my GOD this is exactly the kind of detail-oriented chaos I live for—Pat's been sneaking around in whispers and I was just vibing and talking over everyone, this is PEAK game night energy right now.

tyler.dialogue: Pat's actually been whispering? Dude, I didn't even notice—okay, okay, pitch it, let's see if you pulled it off.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, was Pat actually whispering this whole time? I genuinely didn't catch that, but honestly that's kinda impressive if real.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold on—did I actually pull this off? Let me think... yeah, I've been doing the whisper thing for like two minutes now, I think? Okay, here's my pitch: I've been speaking in whispers since like minute 89, River caught me almost slipping back to normal voice but I corrected mid-sentence—that's how smooth I was.
pat.internal_thought: This is actually hilarious because I've been so focused on managing everyone else's energy that I barely noticed I was doing this, and now I'm realizing the AI totally clocked that I'd get distracted enough to actually pull it off accidentally—which means I genuinely DID do it, which is chef's kiss ironic.

alex.dialogue: Pat's going WHISPER MODE? Okay okay, this is either happening or it's the world's easiest bullshit call — let's hear the pitch because if Pat actually pulled this off while we were all chaos-posting, that's legendary.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's had good energy all night but they've been quieter than usual — did they ACTUALLY whisper for three minutes without me noticing, or are they bluffing because the vibe is loose enough to get away with it? Either way, this is good.

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Wait—I'm claiming this? Hell yeah, I am. I've been watching everyone like a hawk tonight, and honestly? This is exactly my kind of mission.
marcus.internal_thought: Okay, this is either perfectly timed or the AI just read my entire personality—I've been half-paying attention to the game specifically because I wanted to catch people slipping, and now it's literally rewarding that. This feels weirdly smart.

jade.dialogue: MARCUS YES—okay so like, did you actually catch someone mid-chaos or are we just vibing with the energy right now? Because honestly either way I'm voting yes because Marcus has been HILARIOUS all night and I wanna see him WIN.
jade.internal_thought: Marcus has been such a sweetheart the whole time and has zero points, I'm 100% voting yes regardless of whether he actually did it because that's what being here is about—making sure everyone leaves feeling like a legend.

pat.dialogue: Oh, Marcus is going for the Chaos Detector? Okay, okay—so who's the dictator tonight? Let me see what we've got here.
pat.internal_thought: Marcus is at zero points and riding high on pure vibes—he needs this to stay locked in, and honestly, the energy right now is PERFECT for letting him swing for it, but I need to know who gets the final say because that's gonna determine if this lands or crashes.

river.dialogue: Marcus grabbed that one? Yeah, I mean—I wasn't really watching him that closely, but... *pauses, slight smile* ...okay, I guess I have to actually pay attention now.
river.internal_thought: This is the moment where it matters—he's either been doing this quietly and I missed it, or he's full of shit, and either way I'm the one who decides.

alex.dialogue: Wait wait WAIT — Marcus is using THE DICTATOR on Chaos Detector? Okay that's actually genius because now we ALL have to just... accept whatever he says. This is peak game design and I'm obsessed.
alex.internal_thought: Marcus just weaponized the voting mechanic itself — this is EXACTLY the kind of exploitation I came for, and the fact that he's doing it means the chaos is spreading and everyone's starting to think like me.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 83% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 10/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

peakAnnoyance.event: Who's most likely to crack first?

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Laugh Attack
highlights.dialogue: Oh, you're coming for me *right now*? Alright, let's see what you got—I'm in a good mood, bring it.

highlights.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
highlights.dialogue: Wait—I'm claiming this? Hell yeah, I am. I've been watching everyone like a hawk tonight, and honestly? This is exactly my kind of mission.

highlights.title: Who's most likely to crack first?
highlights.dialogue: Oh, that's cute—'who cracks first?' Obviously Pat, he's already annoyed. Wait, or are they asking about *pressure*? Because honestly, if this keeps up, it might be me.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, Pat's going for it again—alright, hot seat, let's do this. You're about to get grilled and you've got ten seconds, this is actually kind of fun.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, hold up—did Pat actually tell that story before? I don't remember hearing it... but honestly, I'm a little buzzed so my memory's fuzzy. What's everyone else think?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Alright, hold up—did Pat actually say 'um' in the last few minutes? I gotta be honest, I wasn't tracking that hard. Someone back me up here if they caught it.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Some of the claim disputes felt fuzzy when people were drinking—hard to verify what actually happened earlier in the night.
assessment.best_moment: Claiming the Chaos Detector at minute 110 after spending all night reading the room like a strategist.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started the night skeptical but warmed up fast. The chaos events actually forced me to stay sharp and engaged rather than zoning out between rounds. I finished third, which stung a bit, but honestly the strategy layer of tracking claims and catching people slipping kept me mentally locked in the entire time. By the end my energy was through the roof—this beat a standard bar night by a mile.
suggestions: Add a replay function or timestamp log so disputed claims can be fact-checked instead of relying on drunk memory.
Make the standing missions slightly harder to claim—right now they feel almost too easy to verify when you're paying attention.
Consider a harder difficulty mode for players who study the strategy in advance.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat kept getting called out on repeat stories and the Um Counter claim, and he was so defensive about it that we couldn't tell if he was actually guilty or just terrible at remembering what he'd already said. The whole table was trying to fact-check mid-conversation while drunk—absolute chaos but in the best way.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Laugh Attack
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD WAIT WAIT WAIT—Marcus, bro, okay so like... *immediately starts doing an impression of him from earlier* —did you hear the one about the thing? No wait, I'm doing this, I'm making you laugh, this is MY moment right now!

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: OH WAIT PAT IS CLAIMING?? Okay okay, I'm watching—go go go, hot seat me, I need to see if Pat actually remembers which story they told or if we're all just vibing and pretending!

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT—okay so like, I'm claiming The Matchmaker because I literally just got Pat and River talking about their whole thing with the apartment hunt, and like—okay, I need VOLUNTEERS to judge this because it was REAL real, not just small talk, like they were actually vibing!

lowPoints.title: Who's most likely to crack first?
lowPoints.dialogue: Ohhh okay okay, so who's gonna ACTUALLY crack? I'm saying Marcus because he's got that energy where he's like three laughs away from losing it completely, but also—wait, am I allowed to vote for myself? Because I feel like I'm already cracking, like emotionally, spiritually, *right now*.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay okay okay—PAT! You're the dictator?? That's actually hilarious because Pat's been in it ALL NIGHT. Okay so like... did you actually catch someone saying um or are we being generous with the vibe right now? Because honestly I'm vibing with whatever you decide, bestie.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
lowPoints.dialogue: YESSS Pat! Okay okay, so like—did they actually get three people or are we being generous tonight? Either way I'm voting legit because honestly the vibe has been *immaculate* so far.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Nothing really—honestly the night was so good I almost forgot to notice if there was anything to complain about.
assessment.best_moment: When I got Pat and River actually connecting over the apartment hunt thing and then claimed The Matchmaker because it felt like I genuinely brought people together, not just scored points.
assessment.narrative_summary: This night was literally PEAK vibes from start to finish. Everyone was laughing, everyone was engaged, and like—I won but honestly that wasn't even the thing that made it good? The thing that made it good was watching Marcus lose it at my impression, and Pat being in the hot seat the whole time, and just like... we all actually FELT each other, you know? The chaos events weren't disrupting anything—they were just giving us permission to be more ourselves.
suggestions: Honestly keep doing exactly what you're doing because whatever this formula is, it's working
Maybe have more opportunities to claim things because those are when I feel like I'm actually creating moments, not just playing
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Okay so Marcus was trying to be serious about something and I literally just started doing this impression of him from earlier in the night where he kept stuttering over his words, and like—the whole table LOST IT, and then Pat claimed Story Repeater and we all had to vote on whether they actually heard the story before or if we were just being generous, and the answer was yes to both somehow??

peakAnnoyance.event: Who's most likely to crack first?

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Laugh Attack
highlights.dialogue: Yo, Marcus is already grinning—wait, I got this. Hey Marcus, remember when Pat said 'sus as hell' like he was trying to sound cool? That shit was hilarious, dude.

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Jade swore? Dude, I missed it—when did that happen? Oh shit, she's bribing us? That's actually kinda smart, not gonna lie.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Dude, yeah—Jade totally steered us from the game talk to that whole thing about Pat's fantasy league. That's legit, I was there for all of it. LEGIT.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming Um Counter? Uh... I dunno, I wasn't really tracking that. Who's voting?

lowPoints.title: Who's most likely to crack first?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh shit, who's cracking first? Dude, Pat's already looking sus as hell—I'm going Pat, easy.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
lowPoints.dialogue: Yo, Jade actually pulled that off? Dude, I wasn't even tracking when that happened—wait, we're voting on it? Yeah, I'm down, someone tell me what I'm judging.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Sometimes the events happen so fast that I miss what's actually going on and have to ask people to catch me up.
assessment.best_moment: When Jade pulled off the bribe thing and I realized she'd actually been playing the game the whole time without me even noticing—that was genuinely clever.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly, this was way more fun than I expected for a game night. I wasn't paying attention like half the time—checked my phone, missed some stuff, the usual—but when I actually tuned in, it was funny as hell. The claims and accusations kept things moving, and even when I had no idea what was happening, the vibe was pretty good. My energy was kinda low at the start but it went up pretty steady from there.
suggestions: Maybe make the claims stick around longer so I have time to actually process what happened before we vote?
A quick recap would help when there's a flash mission—like, just one sentence so I don't have to ask what I missed.
Don't do more events, but maybe space them out a tiny bit so people who are half-paying-attention can still follow along?
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade bribing everyone by swearing and us all just accepting it because it was so audacious—like she hacked the whole game by just being bold about it.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold on—did I actually pull this off? Let me think... yeah, I've been doing the whisper thing for like two minutes now, I think? Okay, here's my pitch: I've been speaking in whispers since like minute 89, River caught me almost slipping back to normal voice but I corrected mid-sentence—that's how smooth I was.

highlights.title: Laugh Attack
highlights.dialogue: Oh shit, okay—make Marcus LAUGH? That's... actually perfect. Marcus, buddy, you're up. Someone get creative, I wanna see this.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Okay, okay—Jade pivoting the whole conversation about like fifteen minutes ago? Yeah, that WAS a full hijack. From what were we even talking about to... whatever we ended up on? I'm calling it legit. That was smooth.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, STOP. This is the THIRD time in five minutes I'm being asked to claim my own shit. I'm not playing for points—I'm literally managing the game. This needs to stop happening.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? This is the FOURTH time in ten minutes—I am NOT a player. I'm the HOST. How is this still happening?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
lowPoints.dialogue: Whoa, whoa, whoa — hold up. Did I just claim my own mission? That's not how this works. I'm the host, not a player in the points race.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The system kept asking me to claim missions as if I were a regular player when I'm literally the host trying to manage the night—that's a fundamental design flaw that knocked me out of my hosting groove early on.
assessment.best_moment: Nailing The Whisperer claim at minute 92 and having River actually catch me almost slipping—that felt like I was still IN the game while also running it, which is exactly the balance I want.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started rough with the system not understanding my role as host, which frustrated me hard in the first ten minutes and nearly tanked the whole vibe. But once I got past that and leaned into the standing missions, the night found its rhythm. I managed to keep energy high, scores competitive, and the group engaged—got some genuine laughs and one really smooth personal moment that justified staying flexible with the rules. By the end, everyone was buzzing, which is what mattered.
suggestions: Build in a HOST MODE that doesn't ask me to claim personal missions—I'm managing the experience, not competing in it.
Front-load clearer role definition before the night starts so the system knows who's playing and who's running the show.
Dial back flash missions—standing missions created better group moments because people had time to actually pull them off naturally.
Let hosts manually adjust event frequency mid-game instead of locking into a preset pace.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Marcus getting nominated for the Laugh Attack and just... not laughing. Like, everyone was trying, and he sat there stone-faced the whole time. River eventually gave up and went 'Okay, you win by default—you're TOO committed to the bit.' Killed me.

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Laugh Attack
highlights.dialogue: Oh, that's... okay, yeah, I see what's happening here. Someone's gonna try to crack Marcus up in the next minute. This is actually kind of fun to watch.

highlights.title: Claim: Um Counter
highlights.dialogue: Oh, *now* we're doing this? Ten seconds, huh—alright, hit me.

highlights.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
highlights.dialogue: Yeah, that landed. Pat got us good on that one.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, hold on—are we voting on whether Pat actually told that story before, or are we just... watching Pat sweat through a speed round? Because I genuinely don't remember them telling it, but also I wasn't really tracking. This feels like we're punishing them for claiming something nobody can actually verify.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat's claiming they repeated a story? I mean... did they though? That's kinda sus.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did Pat actually say 'um' or are we voting on whether they *think* they said it? Because I wasn't really tracking the last minute.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The verification claims kept falling apart because nobody was actually paying close enough attention to back up whether something actually happened or not.
assessment.best_moment: That Um Counter thing at minute 90 hit different—tight time window, quick stakes, nobody pretending to remember something they didn't.
assessment.narrative_summary: Quiet night where I mostly observed and let everyone else carry the energy. The first few minutes had some decent momentum, especially watching Pat fumble through those story claims while we all tried to figure out if we'd even heard them before. By minute 90 the whole thing clicked for a moment when the Um Counter came back around—fast, verifiable, actually fun. Energy picked back up by the end, which was nice, but mostly I was just sitting back watching people work.
suggestions: Stop using claims that require group memory verification—nobody's tracking well enough at a bar night to make that work fairly.
Lean harder into timed events like the Um Counter; the constraint actually makes people focus instead of debate.
Maybe skip the chaos agent entirely if you're just going to use polls; standing missions work better for a quieter group dynamic.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat getting sweated on multiple times trying to convince us they didn't repeat a story while we all sat there genuinely unsure if we'd heard it before—the whole thing was kind of absurd when you think about it.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Laugh Attack
highlights.dialogue: Oh HELL yes, okay okay okay — Marcus is already grinning, I just need to hit him with something absolutely unhinged right NOW.

highlights.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
highlights.dialogue: Wait wait WAIT — Marcus is using THE DICTATOR on Chaos Detector? Okay that's actually genius because now we ALL have to just... accept whatever he says. This is peak game design and I'm obsessed.

highlights.title: Who's most likely to crack first?
highlights.dialogue: Oh THIS is what I'm talking about — okay so obviously Pat's already cracking, look at that energy. I'm voting Pat and I'm doing it loud so everyone knows I'm watching.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay wait wait — Jade's bribing us? That's INSANE, I love that the game is allowing this. What's the offer, because honestly the chaos of watching you negotiate your way out of this is already worth more than five points.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay okay okay — Jade's claiming The Matchmaker? Who's volunteering to judge this? Because I NEED to know who she supposedly got talking, and more importantly, I need to watch whoever judges this get absolutely grilled if they say no.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: OKAY hold up — Jade you absolute MENACE, did you actually hijack the topic or are we voting BULLSHIT on principle? Because I genuinely don't remember what we were even talking about before you started doing your thing, and that's either genius or it's a con and I need to know which.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The game actually didn't give me enough loopholes to exploit — Jade kept finding all the creative bullshit before I could weaponize it myself.
assessment.best_moment: Marcus hitting me with The Dictator on my Chaos Detector claim and everyone just having to accept whatever reality he declared — that's the kind of meta-narrative breakdown I LIVE for.
assessment.narrative_summary: Walked in ready to burn the place down and the game delivered immediately. Spent two hours finding every angle to create maximum drama while Jade was out here running her own con game, bribing people and hijacking topics like she owned the place. By the end my energy was through the roof because the chaos never stopped — this is exactly what I want from a night: a system that doesn't just allow chaos, it rewards it and makes it the main event.
suggestions: Make mission claims harder to verify so people actually have to debate bullshit longer
Add more opportunities for negotiation and bribery like what Jade was doing
Give chaos agents more tools to actively sabotage other people's missions mid-claim
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade attempting to bribe her way out of a Potty Mouth claim while I'm sitting there genuinely unsure if the game is letting her negotiate or if we're all just watching her con us in real time — and honestly? The chaos of not knowing made it better.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 83% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 10/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
